SUBJECT: Detected VPN access that looks like an attack.
DESCRIPTION: Detected VPN access that looks like an attack.  Using eppersons on toughbook laptop m3021107
WKITSM__BACKOUTTIME__C: No Outage and Less Than 1 Hour
WKITSM__DIAGNOSTICS__C:
Tried to call Shaun's mobile phone since he is on vacation this week. Didn't get an answer so I disabled Shaun's AD account and started checking the FW logs. Found three 10.9. IP addresses linked to eppersons username and setup blocks on the FW. Monitored traffic until I was able to get Shaun on the phone. Shaun verified that last night and today was him connecting to the VPN to correct issues while on vacation. The logs that were provided to us had mobile laptop m6021107. The logs were not correct on the name.


The 10.9.0.0 IPs are in a DHCP VPN pool and change many times during the day when the mobile use drops in and out of Verizon wireless coverage the laptops will get a new IP every time they auto reconnect. Internal Network DHCP IPs normally get the same IP each time but Not VPN DHCP IP addresses. This a normal process. This would explain why your logs are showing the mobile laptop name. Sometimes you can do an nslookup on your local PC to the IP 10.9.?.? and it might give you the correct name since the name is not cached on your local PC.
WKITSM__IMPACTDETAILS__C: False alarm.
WKITSM__RESOLUTIONDETAILS__C:
Called Shaun's mobile phone and talked to him directly about the connections and found that all the connections were his. Shaun was on the VPN last night and today to correct issues using RDP with his work laptop that has VPN certificates and two factor.


False alarm.


Root cause for the false alert and here are steps to ensure for next time this issue occurs.


The 10.9.0.0 IPs are in a DHCP VPN pool and change many times during the day when the mobile use drops in and out of Verizon wireless coverage the laptops will get a new IP every time they auto reconnect. Internal Network DHCP IPs normally get the same IP each time but Not VPN DHCP IP addresses. This a normal process. This would explain why the Darktrace logs are showing the mobile laptop name. Sometimes you can do an nslookup on your local PC to the IP 10.9.?.? and it might give you the correct name since the name is not cached on your local PC.


  1. Call the user directly and verify its not the real users traffic.
  2. Check PC name to verify the IP matches to Darktrace logs.
WKITSM__SYMPTOMS__C:
Original Message:


Eliza:

I have an alert here for a toughbook rdping into different servers with shaun credentials but Shaun is saying it wasnt him. Do you think you could by chance hunt down the source of these sessions?

SUBJECT: Direct lightning strike caused backup router issues at FAL DR site.
DESCRIPTION: Direct lightning strike caused backup router issues at FAL DR site.  Backup 10.1.0.2 router failed and will not power up. This is only a backup router.  No users or systems were affected.  Everything was up and running at the primary DC2 site.  All the redundant systems kept everything up and running.
WKITSM__BACKOUTTIME__C: No Outage and Less Than 1 Hour
WKITSM__DIAGNOSTICS__C: Alerts reported the issues with in minutes of the storm. Logged in remotely and checked everything. Went on site and found that the router will not power up. Moved some of the connections to another switch to get some of the management systems back on line.
WKITSM__IMPACTDETAILS__C: The router hosts several devices that monitor, manage and provide communications and POE power to the security cameras.
WKITSM__RESOLUTIONDETAILS__C:
Installed spare switch on site and setup backup routing. Moved all the connections to the spare switch. Contacting support to get the switch replaced. Currently everything is 100% back to production except the security cameras. All 5 security cameras are damaged because of the lightning strike and must be replaced. We are working on getting new cameras setup and installed. To protect the router from another lightning strike we have setup another switch with optical connections to optical isolate the switch in case of another lightning strike. The optical isolation will help prevent power surges from the cameras taking out of devices. None of the users or production system were affected by the lightning strike because of the redundant systems protected everything.


Note: One of the AC units at the FAL site is having issues after the lightning strike. Maintenance is correcting the issue.


Note:The FAL substation building that is completely isolated from the DR site lost a Scada RTU. The RTU was replaced Friday night and all the communications were back online.
WKITSM__ROOTCAUSE__C: Direct lightning strike caused backup router issues at FAL DR site. Backup 10.1.0.2 router failed and will not power up. Looks like lightning came in on one of the outside mounted security cameras. The cameras are directly attached to the router using copper CAT5 cabling. The cameras are power using the POE power from the router.
WKITSM__WORKAROUNDDETAILS__C: Installed spare switch on site and setup backup routing. Moved all the connections to the spare switch. Everything is now back on line.

SUBJECT: enQuesta sending duplicate payments to Omnia
DESCRIPTION: Ever since the enQuesta v6 upgrade, it's been sending duplicate payments in the remittance files to Omnia.
WKITSM__BACKOUTTIME__C: No Outage and Less Than 1 Hour
WKITSM__IMPACTDETAILS__C: Thousands of duplicate payments in Omnia each day. In the end, I cleaned up nearly 47,000 adjustments totaling $5.3 million in duplicate payments.
WKITSM__KNOWNERROR__C: The payments are coming across in two ways. The Paymentus API sends one copy of the payment to Omnia. The duplicate copy is then being pulled in through the enQuesta remittance batches. Because the payments are coming in two completely separate ways, there's no good way to automate checking for these. Given that, I am cleaning up the payments manually.
WKITSM__RESOLUTIONDETAILS__C:
S&S put a fix into production for this on Friday, May 9. The duplicates are still in the API and remittance files, but they're using a new enQuesta table called CSEXTPAYMENTKEY to check for these. If an Omnia payment is in this table as SENT then it's not supposed to be sent again. This feels a bit risky to me, so I'm calling it Workaround Accepted rather than truly resolved.


The Saturday and Sunday remittance files were clean after everything was put in, so perhaps this will work long-term. For now, I'm going to close this out. We'll open another case if it starts reocurring.
WKITSM__RISKDETAILS__C: Deleting payments in the database is exceptionally risky, thus we are moving away from that. The risk now is accidentally deleting valid payments -- we will err on the side of caution, and I will make sure to note the customers who need to be reviewed.
WKITSM__ROOTCAUSE__C:
Essentially, something in the enQuesta v6 upgrade. S&S is aware and says they will have a fix sometime during the week of 4/27. It took a week longer than expected, and was deployed on Friday, May 9.


The original S&S ticket number for this issue was CS-39058, then merged into SU-3295.
WKITSM__SYMPTOMS__C: Customer payments are being duplicated in Omnia -- one comes in through the API, and one comes in through the remittance batch. Without cleaning this up, EPB would be giving away hundreds of thousands of dollars in service each day.
WKITSM__WORKAROUNDDETAILS__C: At first, we didn't realize the problem. The duplicates came in, and I was then deleting the duplicate payments out of Omnia. This is only possible if the batches aren't posted, so it is risky. Instead, we're going to start editing the actual nightly Paymentus remittance batch file. It's created each morning at 4:30 AM. I will compare it to Omnia, and remove the payments already in the system.

SUBJECT: BCBST and USAdmin did not run
DESCRIPTION:
Even though this case is resolved, I receive periodic emails from HR reporting issues with this process. I need to understand what causes these issues, how they are resolved and how resolution steps can be performed by other team members since these are being emailed to Tony.  From: Obermark, Lauren <obermarkle@epb.net>
Date: Friday, March 15, 2024 at 9:30 AM
To: Fister, Krystle <fisterkb@epb.net>
Cc: Poovendran, Partha <poovendranp@epb.net>, Logan, Tony <logantm@epb.net>, Whitaker, Eric <whitakerec@epb.net>, Needham, Adam <Needhamam@epb.net>, Boykin, Chantee <boykince@epb.net>
Subject: Re: PLEASE REVIEW --- BCBST and USAdmin did not run 
Hi all - Have we been able to get the files generated and determine what issue is causing them not to auto generate? 

I’ll reiterate Krystle’s comments that this process is high priority in order to get folks added to our plan. 

In reading through the email chain, I can fill in some answer to some of the questions…  

All these files are generated from Oracle as that is where the employee data and benefit election info is stored.  The files auto save to the O drive on Wednesday mornings.  We have a process in HR to manually review the files and move them to a folder named ‘Send’.  From there, an automated process picks them up and sends them to the applicable vendor.  I am not as familiar with the automated process after they are moved into the ‘Send’ folder, so that’s where Tony can fill in some gaps. 

Also, the config docs for the vendor files should be saved on the O drive.  We did extensive work on the BCBST file, so I’m pretty familiar with that one.  If we need to get together Monday morning when I am back in town to review these configs, please let me know. 

A few questions I have: 
Can we determine if the files generated at all (I.e. is this issue in Oracle or with our process after the files are generated from Oracle)?  
Could this be an issue with the latest Oracle quarterly upgrade?
Why did the other files generate as normal, but not these?
WKITSM__BACKOUTTIME__C: No Outage and Less Than 1 Hour
WKITSM__DIAGNOSTICS__C:
From: Poovendran, Partha <poovendranp@epb.net>

Thank you for the follow up on this. Let me summarize it very short. 

 

This is a report which needs to sent to BCBS for updating the benefits for the retire.

 

  1. This report is used to run on a biweekly basis, The data is taken from Oracle in a form of report (My part) and it sends to some kind of server (Tony’s part), Then this automatically goes to BCBS company.
  2. The reason why this got errored out is because one of the employees had special character specific (Oracle accepted it but got rejected from Tony’s end)
  3. Finally, Tony made his magic and made it work even if it is special character.

 

But Krystle will confirm coming Wednesday if the issue is still repetitive, or it is resolved.

SUBJECT: Switch 10.1.50.131 is down.
DESCRIPTION: Switch 10.1.50.131 is down.
WKITSM__BACKOUTTIME__C: No Outage and Less Than 1 Hour
WKITSM__IMPACTDETAILS__C:
Very little impact. All server are connected to both switches and are 100% up. The redundancy on the network kept everything up and running. Only two management ports are currently down because the management ports only connect to one switch.



To:​IMC Public Reports

​

Thu 2/15/2024 8:48 AM

NMS: 10.8.24.227

Trap of Source: swb10.1.50.131.epb.net(10.1.50.131)

Location: DC2 Data Center

Contact: Admin

Trap Name: The device does not respond to poll packet

Severity: Critical

Trap Time: 2024-02-15 08:48:31

Description: Device "swb10.1.50.131.epb.net" does not respond to Ping packets.

DurationTime: just now
WKITSM__RESOLUTIONDETAILS__C: Connected a serial cable to the switch to review the logs. Found that the fault finder feature on the switch 10.1.50.131 detected "bad-driver  Excessive undersized or giant packets." on Interface LAG 44 ports K21 and K22 from upstream switch 10.1.50.127 and 10.1.50.128 ports 1/3/44 LAG 44. Checked the logs on both switches and didn't see anything in the logs that pointed to any issues. Enabled switch 10.1.50.131 port K21 and K22 LAG 44. The switch is now backup and running and everything looks normal. Changed the Fault Finder settings on switch 10.1.50.131 to alert on warn only. This will make sure the switch doesn't turn off the switch if it sees the same issue again. Reviewing all the logs. Everything is back to normal. System was only down for 20 Minutes. Only two none critical management ports were down. No users or systems were effected. Everything is now back up.

SUBJECT: Opex Troubleshooting Session Needed
DESCRIPTION: Needs assistance getting Creditron support into app-cts-01 to troubleshoot issue
WKITSM__BACKOUTTIME__C: No Outage and Less Than 1 Hour
WKITSM__IMPACTDETAILS__C: Problems with batch
WKITSM__RESOLUTIONDETAILS__C:
RDP'd to app-cts-01.epb.net

Joined support session

Shared screen in Teams so Cassandra could view


Official resolution from Paul Tayler (Creditron support):

Here are the two folders where the OPEX batches are:

 

\\App-Opx-02\Batches\

\\App-Opx-03\Batches\

 

When renaming the .Err folder, you have to take out everything from the first . and on. For the one today it started as:

 

00008781.05082024084903.Err

 

You would need to rename it to:

 

00008781

 

Service Manager will then pick it up and while it’s processing it the folder will look like:

 

00008781.InProcess

 

And once it’s finished it will put the . and the timestamp and then have .Done

SUBJECT: Fiber payments posting exceptionally slowly
DESCRIPTION: Fiber payments from Paymentus are barely trickling into Omnia. This is causing thousands of payments to back up, which makes it look like payments are failing.
WKITSM__BACKOUTTIME__C: No Outage and Less Than 1 Hour
WKITSM__DIAGNOSTICS__C:
We already tried API resets, and fully restarting Omnia twice. Nothing has changed on the CHR side other than that.


Please see related ITSC cases 7603, 7604, and 7655 for documentation on those.
WKITSM__IMPACTDETAILS__C: See above. Fiber payments are not posting in Omnia because the API calls are running so exceptionally slow. This started on Thursday, after the new Paymentus system went live.
WKITSM__KNOWNERROR__C: See attached Word document for screenshots that explain a little more. There's no real "error" as such -- payments just seem to be backing up like a clogged drain.
WKITSM__RESOLUTIONDETAILS__C: S&S fixed the problems with the remittance files this week. They're now populating the tender type (and all other fields) correctly, and the remittance files' payments are no longer blowing up each morning. We've been monitoring this all week, and haven't had any further problems on the fiber side since the fix went in. Given that, I'm going to mark this as resolved.
WKITSM__RISKDETAILS__C: Again, we're unable to correctly see payments posting in Omnia. If we were processing disconnects, customers might get cut off for no reason.
WKITSM__ROOTCAUSE__C: S&S was not including tender types in the remittance files. Apparently the process was also ignoring the "don't resend" flag, and retrying 5 times. The remittance files were having many failures each morning due to this, and it was taking 40 seconds for each one to timeout. Since this was single-threaded, that's why payments were backing up.
WKITSM__SYMPTOMS__C: I think technically payments would post eventually, but they're going to the enQuesta failed queue after timing out due to the number of backed up payments.
WKITSM__WORKAROUNDDETAILS__C:
CIC could theoretically work the payments manually, but it was nearly 4,000 as of Friday, 8/18. We have agreed to *not* process non-pay disconnects until this is resolved, so that no one will get cut off.


As of Saturday morning at 9:30 AM, the queue had managed to clean itself out. This was possibly due to lack of payments coming in on Friday evening and Saturday morning. Not sure what will happen Monday morning.

SUBJECT: after LEAPP, cannot login - connection reset
DESCRIPTION: after the 1st or 2nd reboot after a LEAPP no logins are possible (not even root)
WKITSM__BACKOUTTIME__C: No Outage and Less Than 1 Hour
WKITSM__DIAGNOSTICS__C:
it is interesting that app-esl-24 did not need the relabel.

related to 8758 and 9087
WKITSM__IMPACTDETAILS__C: temporarily cannot access system
WKITSM__KNOWNERROR__C:
After a LEAPP, an engineer sees the following when ssh to server:

client_loop: send disconnect: Connection reset

This has not happened before. It is now a known issue. this "problem" is the fix for it. It is documented in the LEAPP upgrade guide.
WKITSM__RESOLUTIONDETAILS__C:
when, all ssh connections are denied with the error:

client_loop: send disconnect: Connection reset

then, relabel selinux
WKITSM__RISKDETAILS__C: no known risks. this is a straight forward procedure. I happens rarely during/after a LEAPP. it is documented in our LEAPP procedures.
WKITSM__ROOTCAUSE__C:
the system needs relabeling after LEAPP is complete. LEAPP disables selinux.

usually discovered after selinux is re-enabled after a LEAPP.
WKITSM__SYMPTOMS__C:
all ssh connections are denied with the error:

client_loop: send disconnect: Connection reset

.
WKITSM__WORKAROUNDDETAILS__C:
1. boot in to rescue
  2. remount root with rw
  3. set root password
  4. set/touch selinux to relabel

SUBJECT: network unavailable after reboot
DESCRIPTION:
this occurs to windows, linux, and citrix VMs.  After a reboot, the network is not available.  Shaun, Will, Eric, Brandon, and Logan(fiber) have seen this behavior occur on multiple clusters.  

We do not have a way to trigger the issue, it happens randomly.  

In IT Meeting this week, we decided to collect more information when this happens.  Eric Said he would open a ticket with vmware to investigate, also.

When it happens, please add a comment with:  VM name, time, what cluster, what esxi node, and any errors from the vSphere Console.  Basically, collect as much data about the event as you can because the vSphere logs roll over in a matter of hours.  Alternatively, you could open a new "issue", and put the info in that; then attach that case to this one. :)

Thanks
WKITSM__BACKOUTTIME__C: No Outage and Less Than 1 Hour
WKITSM__IMPACTDETAILS__C: network is unavailable to the guestOS .
WKITSM__KNOWNERROR__C:
there are no known vmware cases as of yet.

Eric, If VMWare has more info, please add it.
WKITSM__RESOLUTIONDETAILS__C:
ESXi updates may resolve the issue.


analysis is in progress.

there is no "state" for in-progress for "problem" cases. So, it will have to sit in "new" until we've collected enough data to move forward.
WKITSM__SYMPTOMS__C: when the VM boots up, the vNIC is connected and looks "fine", but the guestOS cannot get to the network.
WKITSM__WORKAROUNDDETAILS__C: currently, there is no way to avoid this. however, when it happens and after the incident information is collected, deactivating the vNIC for the VM in vSphere, waiting a few seconds, then re-activating the vNIC seems to temporarily resolve the issue.
